Petition For Fees And Costs To Guardian Ad Litem And For Discharge
Off Calendar Feb-18-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ernest Der appeared by zoom as guardian ad litem/petitioner in propria persona.

The court questions if it is correct to assume that Mr. Der will not be seeking any further fees in connection with his services as guardian ad litem and expressed concern about the delay. Mr. Der stated that there are a little bit of fees incurred in the guardianship, that he will be petitioning in the guardianship for fees as court appointed counsel for Rowen and that he did not receive examiner's notes on this issue. Mr. Der also stated that he will not be petitioning for fees for preparing this petition, that he filed a declaration which he believes responds to the issues that were raised in the notes, that the delay had to do with the one property coming through Ohio and that he was receiving a lot of notes in the guardianship as far as the accounting. The Petition for Fees and Costs to Guardian Ad Litem is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Cristobal. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order For Payment Of Family Allowance
Off Calendar Sep-28-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ross Madden for and with Miriam Telles.

Mr. Madden presented oral argument citing the Careau case, that they do not need new facts just different facts, that this an uncontested motion like a default prove up hearing, that they provided a spreadsheet of expenses, that nothing ever put them on notice that they needed to supplement except a request from the Probate Examiner which they complied with, that they were relying on P.C. 6542 regarding retroactivity, that the order carved out medical expenses and retroactivity, that they have met the requirements of all of the cases cited, that additional information was not submitted previously as it was never asked for, that the declaration shows that this is a hardship on the family, Ms. Telles is a single parent raising the child of the decedent, that she is also funding a probate procedure in Ohio, cited cases regarding reconsideration, that the ability to reconsider resides in this court and that they are asking for substantial justice to not leave the family in hardship. Ms. Telles stated that all of the facts have been presented, that she is having a hard time understanding why she is not entitled to a family allowance, that she was advised by counsel that the top priority was to be appointed administrator, then reimbursement for estate bills and then the request for family allowance which they thought would be granted retroactively, that there is general frustration with how long this has taken to close, that they are still waiting for the house in Ohio to close and that she should not be penalized for this. Mr. Madden stated that they will be filing for partial distribution and that they will have to do an accounting within the next month or two. The Court requested that a proposed order be submitted no later than September 26, 2016 and that the Motion for Reconsideration of Order for Payment of Family Allowance will be under submission as of that date.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. Court Reporter: Nancy Presant-McDonald, CSR 906, 800-288-3376 (Reported).
